mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals impending economic contraction, triggering corporate earnings revisions downward and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in post-2008 data.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in revolving consumer credit and housing starts indicate a synchronized withdrawal of household balance sheet leverage, which suppresses aggregate demand and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when consumer credit declines but housing remains stable, or forward returns show no statistical difference from baseline during joint contractions.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding its historical trend level indicates supply-side overheating, which historically precedes inventory drawdowns and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where INDPRO exceeds 105, or the signal exhibits zero correlation with index movements during 2010-2019.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic momentum that supports corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline during consecutive positive GDP growth periods, or the rolling Rank IC drops below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real purchasing power and forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when inflation exceeds 2.5% YoY, or the signal shows no incremental predictive power over FEDFUNDS in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid nonfarm payroll growth combined with persistently low unemployment indicates a tight labor market that fuels wage-driven consumption without triggering recession fears, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly positive when both conditions are met, or the interaction term shows zero statistical significance in a 5-day return regression.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts surging above 1.2 million units while revolving credit expands signals a credit-fueled housing boom that historically precedes broader economic acceleration, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are negative or flat when housing starts exceed 1.2 million alongside credit expansion, or the joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline over any 5-year period.
